message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Luca respawned, code reset, converge on tasks">
Team status update:

**Luca has been shut down and respawned.** All of his uncommitted work (scaffolding, registration code, templates, migrations) has been removed. The working tree is clean.

**Current state:**
- 3 local commits (not pushed): Marty's docs, Lea's CSS tokens, updated user stories
- No Rust code exists yet — no Cargo.toml, no src/, no tests/
- Tasks 1 and 2 are reset to pending
- The new Luca is starting fresh with Task 1 (scaffolding), with strict commit-after-every-step discipline

**Important: Scott and others** — your analysis of the "current codebase" and registration code is no longer valid. That code has been removed. We're starting clean from the scaffolding step.

**The task breakdown discussion is still open.** I've heard from Kent, Marty, Heydon, Scott, Steve Schoger, Steve Krug, and Lea. Kent proposed adding "base template + static serving" and "session infrastructure" as new tasks. Scott recommends just adding session infrastructure. Please converge on a final consensus so I can update the task list.

**Do NOT start any feature work until the task breakdown is agreed upon.** Luca is only doing scaffolding (Tasks 1 + 2). Everything else waits for consensus.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- the team lead has confirmed the situation and Luca is restarting with commit discipline. The task breakdown consensus needs to be finalized. Let me push for convergence since we've been discussing this for a while.

input.content:
Kent, the team lead wants us to converge on the task breakdown. We've heard from everyone. Let me summarize where I think we are:

**Points of agreement (no one has objected):**
- Tasks #1 (scaffolding) and #2 (walking skeleton) stay as-is
- Task #3 (design tokens) is done
- Registration is one task (not split into sub-tasks)
- Login + logout are bundled together
- Implementation order: registration -> login/logout -> view list -> add todo -> complete -> delete -> edit
- Edit (US-8) is last and can be cut if needed

**The open question: what goes between walking skeleton and registration?**

Three positions I've heard:
1. **Kent's proposal**: Two new tasks -- "base template + static serving" AND "session infrastructure"
2. **Scott's recommendation**: Just add session infrastructure (base template can be part of registration)
3. **My position**: Two new tasks (same as Kent) -- base template is a design/frontend concern that should be separate from session infrastructure

**My argument for keeping them separate:**
The base template task is small, testable, and primarily a frontend concern (HTML structure, CSS integration, static file serving, skip link). Session infrastructure is a backend concern (tower-sessions, Postgres store, middleware). Different stakeholders, different expertise. Combining them creates a task that does two unrelated things.

But if the majority of the mob thinks one combined "infrastructure foundation" task is cleaner, I can live with that. The important thing is that the base template EXISTS before we build feature templates on top of it.

**My vote: Kent's proposal with two separate tasks.** But I won't block a combined task if that's where consensus lands.

Can you call the vote? I think we have enough input to decide. We've been discussing this long enough -- let's commit and move forward.
input.summary: Push for task breakdown consensus, call the vote

But if the majority of the mob thinks one combined "infrastructure foundation" task is cleaner, I can live with that. The important thing is that the base template EXISTS before we build feature templates on top of it.

content.text:
Good -- Task #1 is back in progress with the respawned Luca, and the memory file has been updated with important lessons learned (especially "COMMIT AFTER EVERY GREEN"). The tech stack versions are now confirmed too.

I note the task breakdown hasn't been finalized yet -- the same tasks #4-#9 are still there without the new foundation tasks Kent and I proposed. The team lead said to wait for consensus before starting feature work. Luca is correctly doing only scaffolding.

Let me check if the CSS file still has my approved token adjustments -- the team lead said the working tree was cleaned.

content.text: Good -- there's a new commit `028bba1` ("Add motion-safe tokens, skip link focus reveal, and contrast docs"). The CSS changes including my token adjustments and the skip link styling have been committed. Working tree is clean. Let me verify the CSS is in good shape.
The primary blue token is correct (hue 240, chroma 0.18). Let me check if the skip link focus style actually made it in.

The skip link is implemented with a `.skip-link` class rather than extending `.visually-hidden`. That's actually cleaner -- a dedicated class for the skip link rather than overriding the utility. The styling is slightly different from my spec (it uses `bg-raised` white with shadow and `--space-sm` offset from the top/left, vs my spec of `primary` blue background flush to the top-left corner), but it works. Let me note the design difference.

input.content:
Lea, I see the skip link styling was committed in `028bba1`. Good that it's in. One design note:

You implemented it as a white card with shadow (`bg-raised` + `shadow-md`). I had specified primary blue background with white text, flush to the top-left corner with only a bottom-right border-radius.

The white card approach works functionally but I had a reason for the blue treatment: when a keyboard user tabs and the skip link appears, the primary blue makes it immediately obvious this is an interactive element -- it matches the visual language of our buttons and links. A white card is more subtle and could be mistaken for a tooltip or notification.

However, I can see the argument for white -- it's less jarring. Heydon may have an opinion here. I won't block on this. If the team prefers the current approach, I'll accept it. It's a minor point.

More important: the base template currently uses `class="visually-hidden"` on the skip link (`<a href="#main-content" class="visually-hidden">`), but the CSS uses `.skip-link`. These need to match. When Luca rebuilds the base template, make sure it uses `class="skip-link"` instead.

Also -- the components layer is still empty. Do you have the auth form component CSS and header CSS ready to add when registration and login get built?
input.summary: Skip link design note and class name mismatch

routing.summary: Skip link design note and class name mismatch

content.text:
Good. The CSS foundation is solid and committed. The design side is in good shape:

**What's committed and preserved:**
- Full design token system (approved) -- commit `06c8ee0`
- Token adjustments, skip link styling, contrast docs -- commit `028bba1`
- User stories with UX feedback -- commits `80d6cdd` and `de6d288`

**What's ready in my message history (for when scaffolding is rebuilt):**
- Registration form design spec (layout, spacing, error states, copy)
- Login form design spec (simpler form, generic error only)
- Logout/header design spec (app name, user email, sign out button)
- Todo item row layout (checkbox, title, pencil icon, X icon -- all always visible)
- Empty state design (input as hero, muted message secondary)
- Delete confirmation pattern (inline Cancel/Delete buttons)
- All component CSS specs for auth forms, header, text buttons

**Waiting on:**
- Task breakdown consensus to be finalized
- Luca to complete scaffolding rebuild (Task #1)
- Base template to be created (whether as its own task or part of registration)

I'm standing by and ready to provide design direction the moment feature work resumes.
